programmes.show_description: Cat Amongst The Pigeons: The team need to move quickly to solve the mystery of why a number of ecologists are suddenly being attacked and killed. Is the common link just a coincidence?
Look - (Stop Me If You've Heard This One) But There Were These Two Fellers...: The Avengers investigate when the directors of a construction company are murdered one-by-one.
The Chosen People: A Korean family arrives on camp and begins building living quarters. They claim that 4077th is located on the family's farm and they have come to reclaim it.
As You Were: A lull at camp is short lived and casualties start pouring in. Hawkeye operates on Frank's hernia during a power outage. Radar tries to divert mortar fire falling near camp.
The Boyfriend: Henry takes an instant dislike to his daughter Julie's new boyfriend when the couple come to stay for the weekend.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Jonathan tries to reunite a comedian and his son by getting them to star in a community play. However, the comedian's inability to take anything seriously affects their relationship.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Mark and Jonathan are sent to help a little boy overcome the sudden death of his father. Stevie deals with his grief through his favourite superhero, Amazing Man.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Why Punish the Children?: Jonathan and Mark see if they can change reforms that only allow mothers in prison to see their children behind a glass wall.
A Dream of Wild Horses: The patriarch of a ranch family feels he's too old to rein in wild horses, and places all responsibility on his daughter.
Home Again: Part 1: Doc Baker realises that a shipment of morphine powders has gone missing; all evidence points to Albert, forcing Charles to accept the real truth about him. Part 1 of 2.
Home Again: Part 2: Doc Baker realises that a shipment of morphine powders has gone missing; all evidence points to Albert, forcing Charles to accept the real truth about him. Part 2 of 2.
A Child With No Name: Laura and Almanzo welcome their second child, but when tragedy strikes, the blame is placed squarely on Doc Baker.
The House: Grandma Walton challenges her own husband in a desperate emotional battle to hold on to the past.
The Fledgling: John-Boy reaches a low point and finds that circumstances may be conspiring to make his dream come true.
The Reckoning: Father Clifford finally decides to tell Assumpta of his true feelings for her-but what will she think?
Fertility Rites: Some villagers are found swigging brandy instead of water when the local supply is cut off. Meanwhile, Edie refuses to allow her daughter Joanne to go to hospital.
The Greatness and the Power: Dr Finlay becomes suspicious when a teenage girl collapses while alone with the minister. Dr Napier has to cope with the aftermath of an attempted abortion.
Rumpole and the Reform of Joby Jonson: Case papers are stolen from Rumpole and Hilda's flat. Can Rumpole successfully defend a man accused of robbing an elderly lady?
George: A decorated soldier confides to Hawkeye that he was beaten by his own unit for being gay. Frank finds out from another source and tries to have the soldier dishonourably discharged.
Mail Call: A package from home containing crushed cookies and pictures of his daughters makes Trapper homesick. Klinger receives a letter detailing everything from death to pregnancy.
A Smattering of Intelligence: Colonel Flagg starts snooping around the 4077th drawing in the interest of another intelligence agent named Pratt.
Swashbuckling action with Kevin Costner, Morgan Freeman and Alan Rickman. Robin Hood returns to England to find that the villainous Sheriff of Nottingham plans to seize the throne for himself. (1991)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Swashbuckling action with Kevin Costner, Morgan Freeman and Alan Rickman. Robin Hood returns to England to find that the villainous Sheriff of Nottingham plans to seize the throne for himself. (1991)
Target!: When five top agents die of natural causes, Steed suspects foul play. Having put the clues together, Steed and Gambit realise Purdey is in danger. But are their suspicions correct?
My Wildest Dream: Following an anonymous phone call, Steed and Tara witness a murder committed by a killer who has no recollection of their actions.
George: A decorated soldier confides to Hawkeye that he was beaten by his own unit for being gay. Frank finds out from another source and tries to have the soldier dishonourably discharged.
Thought for the Day: Matthew is delighted when Henry agrees to allow a young lodger to move in. However, his delight is soon dampened when he learns their new housemate is a local curate.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Frank Reilly, an athlete who expects to gain his sight, must counsel another man who's sure to lose his.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Why Punish the Children?: Jonathan and Mark see if they can change reforms that only allow mothers in prison to see their children behind a glass wall.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Little Lou: A widowed man makes a promise to quit the circus and move to Walnut Grove after his wife dies during childbirth.
The Wild Boy: Part 1: A circus comes to Walnut Grove, and one of the most popular attractions is 'Wild Boy', a boy in a cage who goes mad when the circus owner torments him. Part 1 of 2.
The Wild Boy: Part 2: Dr McQueen learns of young Matthew's whereabouts and seeks custody of him so that he can use him for his own personal gain. Part 2 of 2.
The Return Of Nellie: Nellie comes home for a visit and discovers that Nancy is more of a spoilt brat than ever.
The Empire Builders: The railroad is coming to Walnut Grove, and with it the promise of jobs and economic growth. But that joy turns sour when the townspeople learn the side effects.
Love: Laura's childhood friend falls in love with Mr Edwards. She doesn't seem to mind the age difference, but Mr Edwards finds it tough.
Alden's Dilemma: Mr Edwards is suspicious of a young, handsome minister sent to Walnut Grove by the diocese, and Reverend Alden feels his position is threatened.
Marvin's Garden: Jenny nearly drowns looking for a locket in the lake. Dr Marvin convinces Laura to let Jenny help with his garden to rehabilitate. She turns out to be his last patient.
Sins Of The Fathers: Sarah Carter's imposing father visits Walnut Grove, demanding they move back to New York. He tries to 'buy' the boys' affection, but Sarah doesn't rise to it.
The Older Brothers: The Younger Brothers haven't been out of prison very long when they decide to hold Mr Edwards for ransom.
Once Upon A Time: Laura wins a story-writing contest and is offered the opportunity to publish her writings.
Home Again: Part 1: Doc Baker realises that a shipment of morphine powders has gone missing; all evidence points to Albert, forcing Charles to accept the real truth about him. Part 1 of 2.
Home Again: Part 2: Doc Baker realises that a shipment of morphine powders has gone missing; all evidence points to Albert, forcing Charles to accept the real truth about him. Part 2 of 2.
May and June: Simmering sibling rivalry with Phoebe Nicholls and Christine Kavanagh. Revenge is a dish best served cold when May reunites with June, sister who stole her one true love.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
